data.title: Merck wins FDA label expansion for Welireg in kidney cancer
data.description:
The U.S. Food and Drug Administration on Friday approved a label expansion for Merck’s (MRK) oral HIF-2α inhibitor belzutifan (Welireg) in combination with its anti-PD1 inhibitor Keytruda for renal cell carcinoma, a type of kidney cancer.

According to the FDA, Welireg will be indicated as an adjuvant therapy (following surgical resection) in combination with Keytruda or its injectable version, Keytruda Qlex, for adults with renal cell carcinoma expressing a clear cell component who have undergone prior nephrectomy.

The decision was based on data from the company’s LITESPARK-022 trial, which showed that patients receiving Welireg plus Keytruda witnessed statistically significant gains in disease-free survival compared with those on placebo plus Keytruda.

Welireg was previously approved in the U.S. as a late-line option for adults with advanced clear cell RCC. Merck’s (MRK) bid to expand its label received FDA priority review earlier this year with a target action of June 19.

data.title: Shell plans to sell $1B in wind farms, continuing move away from renewables - Bloomberg
data.description:
Shell (SHEL) is preparing to launch a sale of its offshore wind farms and has hired advisors including Rothschild and PJT Partners to lead the sale, which could fetch more than $1B, Bloomberg reported Friday.

The process could launch as soon as the end of this year, with a sale likely in 2027, according to the report.

The plan to sell the offshore farms would mark a further departure from Shell's (SHEL) former strategy to diversify into green energy, choosing to focus on its higher-returning fossil fuel business; the company has been divesting its European onshore renewables businesses and is reviewing strategic options for its ⁠India-based ​Sprng Energy renewable power ​unit, which it bought just four years ago for $1.5B.

Shell (SHEL) once harbored ambitions to become a major player in renewable power, but those plans were shelved after Wael Sawan assumed the company's leadership in early 2023.

data.title: Sen. Warren calls for wealth, AI tax, as Musk becomes world's first trillionaire
data.description:
Senator Elizabeth Warren (D.-MA) called for a wealth tax and a tax on artificial intelligence on Friday, amid the record-breaking initial public offering of SpaceX (SPCX).

“Elon Musk just became the world's first trillionaire,” Sen. Warren posted on X, which is owned by the Musk-controlled SpaceX. “The typical American household would have to work more than 11 MILLION years to make Elon Musk's level of wealth. We need a wealth tax.”

Replies to her post were divided, as some agreed with her stance, while others took exception to it.

Her tweet came after SpaceX officially became a publicly traded company on Friday. Shares opened at $150 after pricing at $135 apiece. In recent trading, shares changed hands at $164.84, a gain of slightly more than 22%.

Musk's stake in SpaceX is worth roughly $866B, and he retained overwhelming voting control after the IPO, giving him effective authority over SpaceX's strategic direction and board composition.

Earlier on Friday, the Massachusetts lawmaker also said there should be a tax on AI, this time referring to Musk as someone who sees a future where AI replaces half of the workforce.

“Elon Musk sees a future where AI replaces HALF of all jobs,” Warren wrote. “If he’s right, our country’s wealth could get sucked up by a handful of billionaires while workers get left behind. We need to tax AI so Americans share in its growth—and Big Tech pays for the disruption it causes.”

SpaceX, Musk, and Tesla (TSLA), which he also heads up, did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Following SpaceX's offering, shares of Tesla reversed and fell more than 2% on Friday.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Midday Need to Know: SpaceX makes historic debut, Copper rallies & more
data.description:
Wall Street’s major market averages are higher on Friday as traders gear up for the highly anticipated SpaceX (SPCX) initial public offering.

The blue chip Dow (DJI) was +0.9% the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was 0.5%, and the tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.3%.

Now, here are four news stories that broke in the morning to watch out for:

SpaceX makes historic debut: SpaceX (SPCX) shares officially opened on the Nasdaq at $150, an 11% gain from the $135 IPO price, in what became the most valuable company ever to go public with an initial valuation of $1.75T. The offering raised $75B and was oversubscribed by a factor of four, with more than $350B in total orders placed. The debut made Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire, with his stake in SpaceX now worth roughly $866B.

Copper rallies on Iran deal hopes: Copper and copper miners are extending gains Friday, lifted by the prospect of an agreement to end the U.S.-Iran war. President Trump said a deal could be signed within days, and Iran's semi-official Mehr News Agency published a 14-point draft deal that includes a possible reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. Benchmark copper futures traded up 1.5% to $13,690/ton on the London Metal Exchange. Freeport-McMoRan rose 1.6% pre-market after surging 6.8% Thursday, while Southern Copper gained 1.2% following an 8.6% jump in the prior session.

Woodside Energy surges on Exxon takeover speculation: Woodside Energy (WDS)  jumped 6.8% in Friday trading following a Bloomberg report that Exxon Mobil (XOM) is studying the Australian company as a potential acquisition target to expand its presence in liquefied natural gas and Asian markets. Woodside, Australia's largest LNG exporter, has secured long-term sales agreements with leading buyers across Asia, including South Korea and Japan. An LNG-focused deal has become a more serious priority since the Iran war effectively shut the Strait of Hormuz and prompted Asian buyers to seek alternative suppliers.

Nvidia hires new government affairs chief: Nvidia (NVDA) has hired lobbyist Bruce Andrews to run its government affairs office in Washington, D.C. Andrews formerly served as head of government affairs at Intel under former CEO Pat Gelsinger and was Deputy Secretary of the U.S. Commerce Department during the Obama Administration. He starts his new position on Monday, June 15.

data.title: Piper Sandler assumes coverage of five regional banks
data.description:
Piper Sandler assumed coverage of five regional banks, with Arrow Financial (AROW) rated Overweight, while Cass Information Systems (CASS), Fulton Financial (FULT), Medallion Financial (MFIN), and OceanFirst Financial (OCFC) rated Neutral.

Arrow Financial should continue its net interest margin rebound while adding franchise value from a greater commercial loan growth focus in its attractive upstate New York footprint, according to the investment bank.

The trajectory and a recent acquisition drive Piper Sandler's expectation for strong EPS growth.

On CASS, the investment bank said payments fee revenue growth has stagnated on a recession in freight, but dollar volumes are picking up on inflation. "We see synergies in terms of payment rails from the bank, but wonder if it serves as a cap to the valuation, which trends high for banks."

Fulton Financial's steady profitability improvement is fairly valued, asset sensitivity has stepped down, and the company is targeting more M&A.

Meanwhile, Medallion Financial's strong loan growth may take time to build to stronger earnings, steps to lower consumer credit losses could lead to solid risk-adjusted returns, while proxy fights remind of a weaker financial past.

Piper Sandler rated OCFC as Neutral on middling profitability as the company digests the FFIC acquisition.

data.title: Space and satellite stocks tumble after SpaceX IPO
data.description:
A long list of space-related and satellite stocks swung lower on Friday after SpaceX's (SPCX) IPO fired off at a trading level below many of the high-flying expectations of investors.

Notable movers included Virgin Galactic (SPCE) -30%, Rocket Lab (RKLB)  -8%, York Space Systems (YSS) -17%, Planet Labs (PL) -10%, Firefly Aerospace (FLY) -18%, Redwire (RDW) -8%, Satellogic (SATL) -11%, EchoStar (SATS) -12%, AST SpaceMobile (ASTS) -13%, Sidus Space (SIDU) -14%,  BlackSky Technologies (BKSY) -11%, and Intuitive Machines (LUNR) -11%.

Many of those names were bid up over the last week on the expectation of a giant rush of investment into the space-related stocks following the SpaceX (SPACE) IPO. Most of the stocks are off their session lows in the last 20 minutes.

data.title: SEALSQ sets first QSOC satellite launch in Q4, but shares are still in red zone
data.description:
SEALSQ (NASDAQ: LAES) announced on Friday that it plans to launch its first Quantum Security Operations Center (QSOC) satellite on a SpaceX mission in Q4 2026, with plans to expand the network to as many as 100 satellites by 2033.

Built with support from WISeKey’s subsidiaries, the company said the launch is the first step toward building a network of satellites that will provide post-quantum cybersecurity, AI services, and secure communications from space.

The initiative comes as demand for quantum-resistant cybersecurity solutions continues to grow amid rising AI adoption and concerns over future cyber threats.

Founder and CEO Carlos Moreira said the initiative builds on technologies validated through 21 SpaceX satellite missions since 2023.

Despite the long-term opportunity, SEALSQ shares have fallen ~3.83% to ~$3.08 today. Additionally, the stock is down 16.8% YTD versus an 8.02% gain of the S&P 500 (SP500) returns over the same period. 

Seeking Alpha's Quant Ratings system gives the stock a Strong Sell rating with a score of 1.30. Meanwhile, Seeking Alpha analysts rate the stock a Buy with a score of 4.00, while Wall Street analysts have a Strong Buy rating of 4.50.

data.title: Exxon weighing takeover targets including Woodside Energy - Bloomberg
data.description:
Woodside Energy (WDS) up 6.8% in Friday's trading following a Bloomberg report that Exxon Mobil (XOM) is studying the Australian company as a potential acquisition target among options to expand its presence in liquefied natural gas and Asian markets.

Exxon (XOM) has been holding early-stage discussions internally, and Woodside (WDS) is one of several targets the company has been evaluating, according to the report.

Woodside (WDS), Australia's largest LNG exporter, has secured long-term sales agreements with leading buyers across Asia, including in South Korea and Japan, making it an attractive target for companies seeking a foothold in the LNG market.

An LNG-focused deal has become a more serious priority since the start of the Iran war, which effectively shut the Strait of Hormuz and prompted buyers in Asia to seek alternative suppliers outside the Middle East.

Woodside (WDS) and Exxon (XOM) are already partners in the Bass Strait project, with Woodside taking over the operatorship last year.

data.title: Citizens downgrades Broadstone Net Lease, upgrades EPR Properties
data.description:
EPR Properties (EPR) was upgraded at Citizens, while Broadstone Net Lease (BNL) was downgraded as the financial services firm updated its net-lease REIT coverage.

For EPR Properties, Citizens sees "opportunity for a meaningful pickup in deployment, portfolio operating trends have remained resilient, while the movie industry appears poised for a rebound throughout the remainder of 2026, which supports the potential for improved rent coverage."

The stock was upgraded to Market Outperform from Market Perform, with the price target set at $70.

The rating aligns with the Seeking Alpha authors rating of Buy. Wall Street analysts grade the stock as Hold, while the Quant Rating system rates it as Strong Buy.

Meanwhile, Broadstone Net Lease's shares are trading relatively in line with Citizens' prior $21 price target, coupled with leverage trending well-ahead of the sector average, with an expanding development pipeline that is expected to keep this metric elevated for the near term.

BNL was cut to Market Perform from Market Outperform, in line with the SA authors rating of Hold. Sell-side analysts grade the stock as Buy, while Quant sees it as Strong Buy.

data.title: Nvidia hires former Obama official to head its government affairs office
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) has hired lobbyist Bruce Andrews to run its government affairs office in Washington, D.C.

Andrews formerly served as the head of government affairs at Intel (INTC) under former CEO Pat Gelsinger. He was also the Deputy Secretary of the U.S. Commerce Department during the Obama Administration.

"I am super excited to join the incredible NVIDIA team," Andrews said in a post on LinkedIn on Thursday. "NVIDIA is a remarkable company at the center of the AI revolution that is transforming the world's largest industries and profoundly impacting society. I'm looking forward to helping NVIDIA lead the AI revolution and reach new breakthroughs for America and the world."

Andrews left his position at Covington & Burling, an elite law firm, to work for the Jensen Huang-led tech titan. He starts his new position on Monday, June 15.

"It has been a great privilege to be a part of the world-class Covington team and to have the opportunity to work alongside such exceptional, collaborative and deeply collegial professionals," Andrews said.
